political.s: FEC: $307K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 11 executive donors.

labor.s: Via parent company JBS N.V.: 328 federal penalties totaling $621.1M since 2000 (Violation Tracker). Supply-chain signals: KnowTheChain 15/100; CHRB 5.2/26; TVPRA exposure.

environment.s: Via parent company JBS N.V.: environmental violation: $4.8M in federal penalties (Violation Tracker).

animals.s: Via parent company JBS N.V.: Bottom-tier BBFAW 2024 — no evidence of group-wide farm-animal welfare policy. FAIRR Protein Producer Index: High risk across animal welfare, deforestation, antibiotic stewardship, and worker rights. [BBFAW Tier 6 · FAIRR High risk]

wiki.extract: Seara was a daily newspaper published in Bucharest, Romania, before and during World War I. Owned by politician Grigore Gheorghe Cantacuzino and, through most of its existence, managed by the controversial Alexandru Bogdan-Pitești, it was an unofficial and unorthodox tribune for the Conservative Party. Its involvement in politics sparked numerous scandals, the longest of which came during the neutrality period (1914–1916). Strongly anti-Slavic, Seara stood out in that context for supporting the German Empire and Central Powers, and was widely alleged to have been financed by a German propagand

health.s: Via parent company JBS N.V.: FSIS Class-I recalls — JBS / Swift ground beef (E. coli) multiple events. CDC AR Threats: JBS — high use of medically-important antibiotics in cattle. Sources: cdc-ar-meat, usda-fsis-recalls.
